# Per4ML Research Group — Data-Efficient AI/ML for HPC

> Dr. Tanzima Z. Islam is an Associate Professor of Computer Science at Texas State University. She leads the Per4ML laboratory, which develops data-efficient AI and ML methods for intelligent HPC systems. She holds the NSF CAREER Award (2025), the DOE Early Career Award (2022), and the R&D 100 Award (2019). She has secured over $4.6M in research funding from NSF, DOE, AMD, and national laboratories including LLNL, BNL, ORNL, and LBNL. She is also the co-founder of Bangladeshi Women in Computer Science and Engineering (BWCSE).

- Principal Investigator: Tanzima Z. Islam, Associate Professor, Computer Science, Texas State University
- Website: https://per4ml.github.io
- PI website: https://www.tanzimaislam.com
- Google Scholar: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=q56uneAAAAAJ&hl=en
- Research funding secured: $4.6M+ (NSF, DOE, AMD, LLNL, BNL, ORNL, LBNL)

## Research Areas
The group works on, and welcomes collaborators and students in, the following topics:

- Multimodal Representation Learning
- Data-Efficient AI
- Foundation Models
- Heterogeneous Data
- Graphs
- Trees
- Tables
- Text
- Images
- Count Data
- Prediction
- Generation
- Counterfactual Decision Making
- Transfer Learning
- Agentic Systems
- Performance Optimization
- HPC
- Healthcare

## Current Team
- Tanzima Z. Islam (PI)
- Ankur Lahiri (PhD)
- Ashna Nawar Ahmed (PhD)
- Banooqa Banday (PhD)
- Chase Phelps (PhD)
- Elvis Fefey (PhD)
- Mohammad Zaeed (PhD)
- Nawshin Tabassum Tanny (PhD)
- Yugesh Bhattarai (PhD)
- Jaya Sravani (MS)

## Selected Projects
- CESAR Co-Design Project: Comparative Performance analysis — Performance
- CESAR Co-Design Project: Proxy Application Development and Validation — Performance
- Cache Bottleneck Characterization — Cache Analysis
- Data-driven decision making — Optimization
- ECRP+Fractal: Few-Shot Transfer Learning for Performance — Few-Shot Learning, HPC, Performance, Prediction, Transfer Learning
- ECRP: PerfGen: Synthesizing Performance using GenAI — GAN, GenAI, HPC, LLM, Machine Learning, Optimization, Performance
- ECRP: Performance-in-a-Graph (PinG) — HPC, Machine Learning, Performance
- MPI and MPI_T — Performance
- Performance Characterization — HPC, Optimization, Performance
- Performance-Aware Application Development — Machine Learning, Performance
- Ph.D. Thesis: Scalable Checkpoint/Restart — Fault Tolerance, Performance
- Power-Aware Resilience — HPC, Performance
- RECUP: Perf. Reproducibility — HPC, Performance, Reproducibility
- Reliable and Efficient Checkpointing for Cycle-sharing Systems — Fault Tolerance, Transfer Learning

## Publications
- Optimas: An Intelligent Analytics-Informed Generative AI Framework for Performance Optimization (2026). KDD. Authors: Zaeed, Mohammad, Islam, Tanzima Z, Indic, Vladimir
- COMPASS: A Unified Decision-Intelligence System for Navigating Performance Trade-off in HPC (2026). arXiv preprint arXiv:2604.22688. Authors: Lahiry, Ankur, Banday, Banooqa, Bhattarai, Yugesh, Zaeed, Mohammad, Islam, Tanzima Z
- Data-driven analysis to understand GPU hardware resource usage of optimizations (2026). The International Journal of High Performance Computing Applications. Authors: Islam, Tanzima Z, Schutte, Holland, Zaeed, Mohammad, Marathe, Aniruddha
- Attention-Informed Surrogates for Navigating Power-Performance Trade-offs in HPC (2025). NeurIPS ML for Systems Workshop. Authors: Nawar Ahmed, Ashna, Banday, Banooqa, Jones, Terry, Islam, Tanzima Z
- Report of the 2025 Workshop on Next-Generation Ecosystems for Scientific Computing: Harnessing Community, Software, and AI for Cross-Disciplinary Team Science (2025). arXiv preprint arXiv:2510.03413. Authors: McInnes, Lois Curfman, Arnold, Dorian, Balaprakash, Prasanna, Bernhardt, Mike, Cerny, Beth, Dubey, Anshu, Giles, Roscoe, Hood, Denice Ward, Leung, Mary Ann, Lopez-Marrero, Vanessa, others
- Reproducibility for HPC and Distributed Environments: Committees, Nondeterminism, Performance and Workflows (2025). Proceedings of the 3rd ACM Conference on Reproducibility and Replicability. Authors: Sirvent, Raül, Carratalá-Saez, Rocı́o, Gueroudji, Amal, Islam, Tanzima, Pouchard, Line, Taufer, Michela
- A Distributed Framework for Causal Modeling of Performance Variability in GPU Traces (2025). arXiv preprint arXiv:2510.18300. Authors: Lahiry, Ankur, Pokharel, Ayush, Banday, Banooqa, Ockerman, Seth, Gueroudji, Amal, Zaeed, Mohammad, Islam, Tanzima Z, Pouchard, Line

Proceedings of the SC'25 Workshops of the International Conference for High Performance Computing, Networking, Storage and Analysis. - Structure-Aware Representation Learning for Effective Performance Prediction (2025). Concurrency and Computation: Practice and Experience. Authors: Ramadan, Tarek, Pinnow, Nathan, Phelps, Chase, Thiagarajan, Jayaraman J, Islam, Tanzima Z. https://doi.org/10.1002/cpe.70046
- xAMM: “Attention” to Details Improves Cross-Platform Prediction Accuracy (2025). 2025 IEEE 25th International Symposium on Cluster, Cloud and Internet Computing (CCGrid). Authors: Dhakal, Aakash Raj, Islam, Tanzima Z, Dey, Arunavo, Nichols, Daniel, Bhatele, Abhinav, Patki, Tapasya, Scogland, Tom, Yeom, Jae-Seung. https://doi.org/10.1109/CCGRID64434.2025.00067

SC'16: Proceedings of the International Conference for High Performance Computing, Networking, Storage and Analysis. Authors: Islam, Tanzima Z, Thiagarajan, Jayaraman J, Bhatele, Abhinav, Schulz, Martin, Gamblin, Todd

- Cmt-bone—a proxy application for compressible multiphase turbulent flows (2016). 2016 IEEE 23rd International Conference on High Performance Computing (HiPC). Authors: Banerjee, Tania, Hackl, Jason, Shringarpure, Mrugesh, Islam, Tanzima, Balachandar, S, Jackson, Thomas, Ranka, Sanjay

- Reliable and efficient distributed checkpointing system for grid environments (2014). Journal of grid computing. Authors: Islam, Tanzima Zerin, Bagchi, Saurabh, Eigenmann, Rudolf
- Exploring the Capabilities of the New MPI_T Interface (2014). Proceedings of the 21st European MPI Users' Group Meeting. Authors: Islam, Tanzima, Mohror, Kathryn, Schulz, Martin
- Lossy Compression for Checkpointing: Fallible or Feasible? (2014). International Conference for High Performance Computing, Networking, Storage, and Analysis. Authors: Ni, Xiang, Kale, Laxmikant, Islam, Tanzima, Mohror, Kathryn, Moody, Adam
- Batchsubmit: a high-volume batch submission system for earthquake engineering simulation (2014). Concurrency and Computation: Practice and Experience. Authors: Mohan, Anup, Hacker, Thomas, Rodgers, Gregory P, Islam, Tanzima
- XSBench -- the development and verification of a performance abstraction for Monte Carlo reactor analysis (2014). Proceedings of PHYSOR 2014, Kyoto, Japan, Sep 28 -- Oct 3, 2014. Authors: Tramm, John R, Siegel, Andrew R, Islam, Tanzima, Schulz, Martin
- Reliable and Scalable Checkpointing Systems (2013). Authors: Islam, Tanzima Zerin
- McrEngine: a scalable checkpointing system using data-aware aggregation and compression (2012). Proceedings of the International Conference on High Performance Computing, Networking, Storage and Analysis. Authors: Islam, Tanzima Zerin, Mohror, Kathryn, Bagchi, Saurabh, Moody, Adam, de Supinski, Bronis R., Eigenmann, Rudolf
- Eunomia: A fast and collaborative interference avoidance technique for wireless medical devices (2011). Authors: Hossain, Mohammad S, Islam, Tanzima Zerin, Wei, Jinliang, Raghunathan, Vijay, Bagchi, Saurabh
- FALCON: a system for reliable checkpoint recovery in shared grid environments (2009). Proceedings of the Conference on High Performance Computing Networking, Storage and Analysis. Authors: Islam, Tanzima Zerin, Bagchi, Saurabh, Eigenmann, Rudolf. https://doi.org/10.1145/1654059.1654110
- Gpnocsim - A General Purpose Simulator for Network-On-Chip (2007). 2007 International Conference on Information and Communication Technology. Authors: Hossain, Hemayet, Ahmed, Mostak, Al-Nayeem, Abdullah, Islam, Tanzima Zerin, Akbar, Md. Mostofa
- Development of an Improved General Purpose Simulator for Network-On-Chip (2006). Authors: Al-Nayeem, Abdullah, Islam, Tanzima Zerin

## For prospective students & collaborators
The lab welcomes PhD applicants, MS students, and undergraduate researchers interested in the research areas above. Reach out via the PI's website: https://www.tanzimaislam.com
